Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]]

Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:信濃国の式内社]]

{{ill|Q135039931|ltq=Q135039931|WD=Q135039931|1=Shiratamatarihono- Shrine}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長野県長野市吉田一丁目12番', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長野県長野市富田', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長野市の神社]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]

[[JA:皇足穂吉田大御神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q11581101}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū |Image = [[File:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}{{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, No. 12 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}・{{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternate names = Yoshida-no-miya, Yoshida Shrine |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=September 25|Q2886|WD=Q2886|1=September 25}} |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|label=Sumetaruo
Yoshida Ōmi
kamigū|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū''' (すめたるほよしだおおみかみぐう) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} (a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}) located in {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Nagano City|Q128849|WD=Q128849|1=Nagano (city)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It is commonly known as '''Yoshida Shrine'''. === Overview === As its name appears in the shrine register (Jinmyōchō) of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} from {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5), the shrine has a long history. Originally, it was enshrined about 1 km to the east in Komachi (present-day {{ill|lt=Yoshida (Nagano City)|Q11413505|WD=Q11413505|lt=Yoshida|1=Yoshida}} 3-chōme). However, because the area was frequently flooded by the {{ill|lt=Asakawa River (Nagano)|Q11557083|WD=Q11557083|lt=Asakawa|1=Asa River}} and because the {{ill|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō (Shin’etsu)|Q3069767|WD=Q3069767|lt=Hokkoku Kaidō|1=Hokkoku Kaidō}} was newly developed, the shrine was relocated during the {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era to its current site along the Hokkoku Kaidō (Uemachi, present-day Yoshida 1-chōme).[http://weekly-nagano.main.jp/2010/06/97.html 097 Yoshida Shrine—The lively precincts during the Festival of the Gods] - Weekly Nagano At the time of this relocation, nearby shrines were consolidated, resulting in the unusual configuration of approximately 100 auxiliary shrines of various sizes within the precincts. The sacred {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=goshinboku|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} (described below) that stood at the former site was left in place even after the relocation and remains vigorous to this day. === The Yoshida Ginkgo === The {{ill|lt=ginkgo|Q43284|WD=Q43284|1=Ginkgo biloba}} tree that still remains at the former shrine site is estimated to be 900 years old, with a height of 32 m, a trunk circumference at eye level of 8.6 m, and a branch spread of 11–15 m—an enormous tree.[http://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/uploaded/attachment/5599.pdf Yoshida Ginkgo　Ginkgo tree in Yoshida] - Cultural Properties Division, Board of Education, Nagano City Before World War II it was designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture, and it is currently designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. It is a male tree and does not bear fruit, but according to legend, if its bark is soaked in water and the water is given to a mother to drink, her {{ill|lt=breast milk|Q22728|WD=Q22728|1=Breast milk}} will flow. For this reason, it is called the “Milk Ginkgo.” [[File:皇足穂吉田大御神宮 - panoramio.jpg|thumbnail|The Great Ginkgo of Yoshida]] === History === * {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5) – '''Sumetaruo-no-miya''' is listed among the nine shrines of {{ill|lt=Minochi District|Q11548296|WD=Q11548296|1=Minochi district}} in the shrine register of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} era – The shrine site is moved from Komachi (present-day Yoshida 3-chōme) to Uemachi (present-day Yoshida 1-chōme), its current location. * {{ill|lt=1819|Q6967|WD=Q6967|1=1819}} ({{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 2) – The shrine name is changed to '''Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū'''. * {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 10) – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” at the former site is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nagano Prefecture. * {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} (Shōwa 42) {{ill|lt=November 1|Q2997|WD=Q2997|1=November 1}} – The “Yoshida Ginkgo” is designated a Natural Monument of Nagano City. {{section stub}} === Shrine grounds === * {{ill|lt=Main hall|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} * Auxiliary shrines within the precincts ** Branch shrines of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} (13 shrines) ** {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} shrines from across the country (68 shrines) ** Guardian shrines of the local neighborhoods (10 shrines) ** Others (9 shrines) === Access === ; Current site * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kirihara Station (Nagano)|Q11537186|WD=Q11537186|lt=Kirihara Station|1=Kirihara Station (Nagano)}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagano Electric Railway|Q6958437|WD=Q6958437|1=Nagano Electric Railway}} ; Former site (Yoshida Ginkgo) * 3-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Shinano-Yoshida Station|Q11386162|WD=Q11386162|1=Shinano-Yoshida Station}} on the Nagano Electric Railway * 5-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Kita-Nagano Station|Q6417571|WD=Q6417571|1=Kita-Nagano Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Shinano Railway|Q11254401|WD=Q11254401|1=Shinano Railway}} === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Nagano Prefecture}} {{Default sort:Sumetaruo Yoshida Ōmikamigū}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sumetaruho Yoshida-ōkamigū]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shinano Province]]
